title: rubus hirtus var. plusiacanthus
intro: Rubus hirtus var. plusiacanthus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a species of bramble native to the Himalayas. Its medicinal properties lie in its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, colds, and digestive issues. The plant is rich in vitamins and minerals, offering health benefits such as boosting the immune system, improving digestion, and reducing oxidative stress. The herbal preparations of Rubus hirtus var. plusiacanthus include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed fresh or dried, and are often combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
